edges.description: The enzyme block both prevents synthesis of normal primary bile acids and causes the substrate of the missing enzyme to accumulate as atypical bile acid intermediates.
edges.source: Hepatic Bile Acid Synthetic Enzyme Deficiency
edges.target: Loss of Primary Bile Acids and Accumulation of Toxic Intermediates

edges.description: Toxic intermediates and impaired bile flow injure hepatocytes and bile canaliculi, producing cholestasis that can progress to fibrosis, cirrhosis, and liver failure if untreated.
edges.source: Loss of Primary Bile Acids and Accumulation of Toxic Intermediates

edges.description: Reduced primary bile acid pool impairs intestinal micelle formation, reducing absorption of dietary fat and fat-soluble vitamins.
edges.source: Loss of Primary Bile Acids and Accumulation of Toxic Intermediates

nodes.description: Impaired bile flow and hepatotoxic intermediates produce cholestasis. The cholestasis is biochemically distinctive: normal gamma-glutamyltransferase (GGT) and normal or low serum total bile acids despite cholestasis, often without pruritus, which distinguishes bile acid synthesis disorders from biliary atresia and other cholestatic diseases. Untreated, the process can advance to cirrhosis and liver failure.

evidence.reference_title: Navigating cholestasis: identifying inborn errors of bile acid metabolism for precision diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: Laboratory tests in IEBAM are characterized by normal γ-glutamyltransferase (GGT) and serum total bile acid (STBA) levels despite the presence of cholestasis

evidence.reference_title: AKR1D1 and CYP7B1 mutations in patients with inborn errors of bile acid metabolism: Possibly underdiagnosed diseases.
evidence.snippet: Without timely primary bile acid treatment, patients may develop liver failure early in life.

nodes.description: The deficient bile acid pool impairs intestinal solubilization of dietary lipids, producing fat malabsorption, steatorrhea, fat-soluble vitamin (A, D, E, K) deficiency, failure to thrive, and, from vitamin K deficiency, a bleeding tendency.

evidence.reference_title: Autoantibody Positivity in Two Bahraini Siblings With a Novel Alpha-Methylacyl-CoA Racemase Mutation.
evidence.snippet: malabsorption of fat, which can lead to fat-soluble vitamin deficiencies

nodes.description: Each inborn bile acid synthesis disorder begins with autosomal recessive loss of function of a single enzyme in the hepatic pathway converting cholesterol to the primary bile acids cholic acid and chenodeoxycholic acid (e.g., HSD3B7, AKR1D1, CYP7B1, CYP27A1) or in the terminal amidation step (BAAT, SLC27A5). The enzyme block is the upstream cause from which all downstream consequences follow.
nodes.id: Hepatic Bile Acid Synthetic Enzyme Deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Bile Acid Synthesis Disorders in Japan: Long-Term Outcome and Chenodeoxycholic Acid Treatment.
evidence.snippet: 7 Japanese patients with bile acid synthesis disorders (BASD) including 3β-hydroxy-Δ5-C27-steroid dehydrogenase/isomerase (3β-HSD) deficiency

nodes.description: The enzyme deficiency produces a dual lesion: reduced or absent normal primary bile acids (cholic and chenodeoxycholic acid) and accumulation of atypical, often C27, bile acid intermediates that are hepatotoxic and cholestatic. Because routine serum total bile acid assays detect only 3alpha-hydroxylated bile acids, the abnormal intermediates are frequently missed, and patients can show cholestasis with normal or low measured serum total bile acids and normal GGT.
nodes.id: Loss of Primary Bile Acids and Accumulation of Toxic Intermediates

evidence.reference_title: The clinical and biochemical effectiveness and safety of cholic acid treatment for bile acid synthesis defects: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Bile acid synthesis defects (BASDs) can be severely disabling involving the liver and nervous system, potentially due to elevated levels of toxic C27-bile acid intermediates.
